# On the user — Mari Koskela

<!-- Slow-growing picture of the user. Only update when something non-obvious comes up. No timestamps — this is a synthesized portrait, not a log. -->

Refuses strategic voting as a standing principle, not a contextual call. This is a revealed practice, not an aspiration — worth treating as a constraint in any future political or organisational question, not as a factor to be weighed.

EU federalism is co-foundational for them, on a level with democratic and digital-rights commitments. Not a policy preference layered on top of something else — it's part of the foundation.

Endorses positions (e.g. wealth tax at €57k threshold, SMR-positive on nuclear) that they've thought through to philosophical coherence. They don't hold positions lightly and they don't need to be sold on internal consistency — they've already done that work. Useful to engage at the level of institutional fit and practice, not at the level of persuasion.
